---
title: "Teaching Evaluations"
---

## Evaluation Trends Over Time

```{r}
#| label: fig-evals
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-cap: |
#|   Teaching-evaluation trends over time. The three rating panels share a dual
#|   y-axis: the **left axis** is the UCLA TA 9-point Likert scale, the **right
#|   axis** is the UC Davis IOR 5-point scale, and the two are linearly
#|   aligned. UC Davis responses (originally on the 5-point scale) are
#|   stretched onto the 9-point axis so both sets of distributions sit in the
#|   same coordinate space — e.g. a UC Davis rating of 4 plots at 7 on the
#|   left axis, which the right axis correctly reads as 4. **Violins show the
#|   kernel density of individual respondent ratings** (a measure of
#|   student-to-student spread), built from the per-rating counts in each
#|   summary PDF. Lines and points show the mean trend; point size is
#|   proportional to the number of respondents (also labeled `n=...`), and
#|   **opacity is proportional to the response rate** (respondents ÷
#|   enrolled). The three rating items use different Likert anchors and
#|   aren't directly comparable across panels. The x-axis is treated as
#|   discrete so the 2018–2022 gap is collapsed.

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

rds_path <- "data/evals_data.rds"
if (!file.exists(rds_path)) {
  stop(
    "Evaluation data file not found at: ", rds_path, "\n",
    "Please run the build script to generate it:\n",
    "  ./build.sh\n",
    "Or generate it manually:\n",
    "  Rscript data/build_evals_data.R"
  )
}
evals_data <- readRDS(rds_path)
evals      <- evals_data$summary
responses  <- evals_data$responses

# Both data sets get a factor year so the 2018–2022 gap is spliced out.
year_levels    <- as.character(sort(unique(evals$year)))
evals$year_f   <- factor(as.character(evals$year), levels = year_levels)

# Lift every rating onto the UCLA 9-point axis: 9pt = 1 + (5pt - 1) * 2.
responses$rating_9 <- ifelse(
  responses$scale_max == 5,
  1 + (responses$rating - 1) * 2,
  responses$rating
)
# Expand counts into one row per respondent so geom_violin doesn't take the
# weighted-quantile code path (which warns "quantiles for weighted data is
# not implemented").
responses <- responses %>%
  uncount(count) %>%
  mutate(year_f = factor(as.character(year), levels = year_levels)) %>%
  left_join(evals[, c("year", "role", "response_rate", "n_responses")],
            by = c("year", "role"))

role_colors <- c("UC Davis (Instructor of Record)" = "#2780E3",
                 "UCLA (TA)" = "#E67E22")

five_to_nine <- function(x) 1 + (x - 1) * 2
nine_to_five <- function(x) 1 + (x - 1) * 4 / 8

# patchwork's plot_layout(guides = "collect") only merges guides that are
# *structurally identical* across plots — including their computed limits.
# Pin limits/breaks explicitly so the size/alpha legends match.
n_range <- range(evals$n_responses)
shared_role_scales <- list(
  # The fill guide is suppressed because some plots (response rate) don't
  # have a fill-using geom, which would otherwise prevent patchwork from
  # merging the Role legend across plots. Color + shape are enough.
  scale_fill_manual(values = role_colors, guide = "none"),
  scale_color_manual(values = role_colors, name = "Role",
                     guide = guide_legend(order = 1)),
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "Role", guide = guide_legend(order = 1)),
  scale_size_continuous(
    range = c(2.5, 5), limits = n_range,
    breaks = seq(n_range[1], n_range[2]),
    name = "Respondents", guide = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ),
  scale_alpha_continuous(
    range = c(0.25, 1), limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
    labels = scales::percent, name = "Response rate",
    guide = guide_legend(order = 3, override.aes = list(size = 4))
  )
)

rating_plot <- function(metric, title, right_axis_name) {
  resp <- responses[responses$metric == metric, ]
  summ <- data.frame(
    year_f        = evals$year_f,
    role          = evals$role,
    mean_9        = five_to_nine(evals[[metric]]),
    n             = evals$n_responses,
    response_rate = evals$response_rate
  )

  ggplot() +
    geom_violin(
      data = resp,
      aes(x = year_f, y = rating_9, group = interaction(year_f, role),
          fill = role, alpha = response_rate),
      width = 0.75, scale = "width", colour = NA, bw = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = summ,
      aes(x = year_f, y = mean_9, group = role, color = role,
          alpha = response_rate),
      linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = summ,
      aes(x = year_f, y = mean_9, color = role, shape = role,
          alpha = response_rate, size = n)
    ) +
    geom_label(
      data = summ,
      aes(x = year_f, y = mean_9, color = role, label = paste0("n=", n)),
      vjust = -0.8, hjust = 0.5, size = 3.2, alpha = 1,
      fill = "white", label.size = 0.3, label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),
      label.r = unit(0.1, "lines"), fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      name = "UCLA (9-pt)",
      breaks = 1:9,
      sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = nine_to_five,
                          name = right_axis_name, breaks = 1:5)
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 9), clip = "off") +
    shared_role_scales +
    labs(title = title, x = "Year") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 16, r = 8, b = 4, l = 4)
    )
}

response_rate_plot <- function() {
  df <- evals %>% transmute(year_f, role, response_rate, n = n_responses)
  # Response rate isn't mapped to alpha here — the y-axis already shows
  # it, and dropping alpha from this plot lets patchwork merge the alpha
  # guide cleanly across the three rating plots.
  ggplot(df, aes(x = year_f, y = response_rate, color = role, shape = role)) +
    geom_line(aes(group = role), linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(size = n)) +
    geom_label(aes(label = paste0("n=", n)),
               vjust = -0.8, hjust = 0.5, size = 3.2, alpha = 1,
               fill = "white", label.size = 0.3,
               label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),
               label.r = unit(0.1, "lines"), fontface = "bold",
               show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
    shared_role_scales +
    labs(title = "Response rate", x = "Year", y = "Respondents ÷ enrolled") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 16, r = 8, b = 4, l = 4)
    )
}

p_overall      <- rating_plot("overall_rating",           "Overall rating",
                              "UC Davis (1 = Poor … 5 = Excellent)")
p_instructor   <- rating_plot("instructor_effectiveness", "Instructor effectiveness",
                              "UC Davis (1 = Poor … 5 = Excellent)")
p_organization <- rating_plot("course_organization",      "Course well-organized",
                              "UC Davis (1 = Strongly Disagree … 5 = Strongly Agree)")
p_response     <- response_rate_plot()

wrap_plots(p_overall, p_instructor, p_organization, p_response,
           ncol = 2, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.box = "vertical")
```

## UC Davis (Instructor of Record)

### Epi 204

#### 2025

- [Epi 204 Evaluations Summary (2025)](files/evals/evals%20epi%20204%202025%20summary.pdf)

#### 2024

- [Epi 204 Evaluations Summary (2024)](files/evals/Evaluations%20epi%20204%202024%20summary.pdf)

#### 2023

- [Epi 204 Evaluations (Spring 2023)](files/evals/Epi%20204%20Evaluations%20Spring%202023.pdf)

## UCLA (Teaching Assistant)

### Winter 2017

- [Teaching Evaluation 1](files/evals/Morrison%20teaching%20eval%201.pdf)
- [Teaching Evaluation 2](files/evals/Morrison%20teaching%20eval%202.pdf)
